title: Parameters and return values

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific C: Beginner to Intermediate draft for Parameters and return values; human standards, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: GNU C Language Introduction and Reference Manual; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Parameters and return values
sources.claim: C passes argument values into parameter objects; a pointer value can let a function access caller-owned objects, but the pointer itself is still passed by value.

canonicalExplanation.summary: C passes argument values into parameter objects; a pointer value can let a function access caller-owned objects, but the pointer itself is still passed by value.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A swap function receives pointers to two int objects and writes through them, while reassigning only its local pointer parameters would not change caller pointers. Your goal is to predict which caller-visible values change across a function call. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: while, do and for; pf abstraction calls; Declarations, prototypes and definitions. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: C passes argument values into parameter objects; a pointer value can let a function access caller-owned objects, but the pointer itself is still passed by value. This rule is applied to concrete C source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Array parameters adjust to pointers, object lifetime and const qualification constrain access, and aliasing can make writes through two pointers interact. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: C passes variables by reference whenever their names are used as function arguments.

includes: Apply C's value-passing model and use pointer parameters only with clear contracts.
Predict which caller-visible values change across a function call.
excludes: Array parameters adjust to pointers, object lifetime and const qualification constrain access, and aliasing can make writes through two pointers interact.

outcomes: Predict which caller-visible values change across a function call.

examples.title: Parameters and return values: defined case
examples.situation: A swap function receives pointers to two int objects and writes through them, while reassigning only its local pointer parameters would not change caller pointers.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: A swap function receives pointers to two int objects and writes through them, while reassigning only its local pointer parameters would not change caller pointers.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: C passes by value; pass an address explicitly when the function must access caller-owned storage, then validate lifetime and nullability.
examples.result: The defined result follows because C passes by value; pass an address explicitly when the function must access caller-owned storage, then validate lifetime and nullability.

examples.title: Parameters and return values: boundary case
examples.situation: A read-only sum function accepts const int * plus a size_t count and cannot modify elements through that pointer.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: A read-only sum function accepts const int * plus a size_t count and cannot modify elements through that pointer.

Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Array parameters adjust to pointers, object lifetime and const qualification constrain access, and aliasing can make writes through two pointers interact.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: C passes variables by reference whenever their names are used as function arguments.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Predict which caller-visible values change across a function call.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: C passes variables by reference whenever their names are used as function arguments.
misconceptions.correction: C passes by value; pass an address explicitly when the function must access caller-owned storage, then validate lifetime and nullability.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using concrete C rules, explain why this belief fails: C passes variables by reference whenever their names are used as function arguments.

analogy.example: A manually managed workshop can model Parameters and return values: every tool, address, buffer, and acquired resource has an explicit valid range and owner.
usefulFor: Making low-level state, bounds, representation, ownership, and cleanup responsibilities concrete
limitations: C abstract-machine rules, undefined behavior, pointer provenance, translation, and implementation variation are stricter than a physical workshop analogy.
Array parameters adjust to pointers, object lifetime and const qualification constrain access, and aliasing can make writes through two pointers interact.

recap.summary: You should now be able to predict which caller-visible values change across a function call. Keep this boundary in view: Array parameters adjust to pointers, object lifetime and const qualification constrain access, and aliasing can make writes through two pointers interact.
